title: Spectrophotometric Study of the Reaction of Osmium with Promethazine Hydrochloride
author: H. SANKE GOWDA
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Post-graduate Studies and Research in Chemistry, University of Mysore, Manasa Gangotri, Mysore. 

  Manuscript received 14 October 1976 ; accepted 21 March 1977  

 Promethazine hydrochloride is proposed as a new reagent for the spectrophotometric determi­nation of microgram amounts of osmium. It forms a red coloured species with osmium(VIII) or osmium(VI) at room temperature in hydrochloric acid medium. The red species exhibits maximum absorbance at 515 nm. Beer's law is valid over the concentration range, 0 2-4.8 ppm for osmium(VIII) and 0.16-6.0 ppm for osmium(VI). The Sandell's sensitivity of the reaction is 0.021  \(\mu\) g/cm 2  for osmium(VIII) and 0.015 \(\mu\) g/cm 2   for osmium(VII). The effects of time, temperature, acidity, order of addition of reagents, reagent concentration and diverse ions are reported.
